page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 vector extensions, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions.

This server configuration does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached Premium Storage for persistent disk operations. The lack of local storage and GPU hardware positions the D64ls_v5 as a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high CPU density and memory bandwidth without the overhead of specialized accelerators or local NVMe drives. The resource allocation is highly optimized for compute-heavy tasks where memory capacity is secondary to raw processing power.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61847.21 and a PassMark CPU score of 60731.27. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, reaching 292.88 GB/sec for large-block read operations. The server excels in parallel processing tasks, demonstrating strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside a high Redis throughput of 61.81 million SET operations per second. However, single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks. This profile makes the D64ls_v5 highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, software build pipelines, and relational database hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v5 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the DLSv5 family, featuring 64 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 physical cores, this x86_64 server utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, making it a cost-efficient choice for CPU-intensive workloads. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 60731.27 and high memory bandwidth of 292.88 GB/sec for large-block reads. It is highly capable in parallel processing tasks, including software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, though it exhibits average single-core and weak large-model LLM text generation performance. The D64ls_v5 is best suited for multi-threaded applications, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v5 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v5 is a compute-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring sixty-four virtual cores and balanced memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors at 2.8 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU, optimized for remote Premium Storage
Excellent throughput for static web serving and Redis operations
